subject: Physics
title: Sound as a Weapon
description: A short lesson to help you apply Sound as a Weapon.

hook.text: Long Range Acoustic Devices can project sound so intense it causes pain and disorientation at 300 meters.

buildup.text: LRADs focus sound into a narrow beam, concentrating energy the way a laser focuses light.

discovery.text: At full power, they produce over 160 dB—enough to cause immediate hearing damage and extreme discomfort.

twist.text: They were originally designed for ship-to-ship communication but were repurposed for crowd control.

climax.text: Some countries use infrasound weapons that cause nausea and confusion without visible injury.

punchline.text: Sound you can't ignore and can't escape. The invisible weapon.

quiz.question: How do Long Range Acoustic Devices (LRADs) work?
options: They focus intense sound into a narrow directional beam
They emit low-frequency vibrations through the ground
They use electromagnetic pulses disguised as sound
